title: Statistical methods for assessing measurement error (reliability) in variables relevant to sports medicine
notes: Separates bias and random error; CV SEM and limits of agreement require protocol-specific scale assumptions; supports error-aware level-3 claims only.

title: Measures of reliability in sports medicine and science
population: sports medicine and sport science populations
notes: Typical-error framework; study-level reliability sample guidance is not a universal within-athlete baseline count.

title: Quantifying test-retest reliability using the intraclass correlation coefficient and the SEM
notes: ICC model and SEM calculation provenance are required; MDC confidence level must be explicit.

title: Validity, reliability and sensitivity of measures of sporting performance
notes: Validity reliability and sensitivity are separate; a reliable proxy cannot support a claim if it does not represent the target construct.

title: Consensus on measurement properties and feasibility of performance tests for the exercise and sport sciences: a Delphi study
notes: Taxonomy covers validity reliability responsiveness interpretability and feasibility; consensus does not validate a specific metric or set a universal n.

title: A Statistical Framework to Interpret Individual Response to Intervention: Paving the Way for Personalized Nutrition and Exercise Prescription
notes: Combines typical error confidence intervals and SWC; error exceedance and worthwhile magnitude remain distinct and noncausal.

title: Individual response to exercise training - a statistical perspective
notes: Measurement error and within-subject efficacy variation confound responder labels; stable personal response requires replicated interventions or contrasts.

title: A Systematic Review Examining the Approaches Used to Estimate Interindividual Differences in Trainability and Classify Individual Responses to Exercise Training
notes: Response rates inflate when classification ignores error and SWC or MCID; heterogeneous reviewed outcomes prevent universal thresholds.

title: Mastering variation: variance components and personalised medicine
event: variance components and replicated individual contrasts
notes: Methodological opposition to naive personalization; observed between-person spread does not prove repeatable personal treatment response.

title: "Magnitude-based inference": a statistical review
notes: MBI reduced-sample claims are not justified; use transparent intervals or a fully specified Bayesian model while separately justifying important effects.

title: The Problem with "Magnitude-based Inference"
event: MBI type-I and type-II error simulation
notes: MBI can inflate type-I error at small samples; a likely-worthwhile label cannot substitute for a valid uncertainty model.

title: CONSORT extension for reporting N-of-1 trials (CENT) 2015 Statement
population: individual patients in prospective N-of-1 trials
notes: Official PubMed record links an erratum; reporting completeness does not establish design sufficiency safety or sport transfer.

title: Design and Implementation of N-of-1 Trials: A User's Guide
population: patients clinicians and N-of-1 trial designers
event: randomized periods repeated assessment onset carryover and washout
notes: Period length and washout are intervention-specific; training carryover may be the intended adaptation and cannot be replaced by a universal period rule.

title: The Single-Case Reporting Guideline In Behavioural Interventions (SCRIBE) 2016 Statement
notes: Requires phase outcome fidelity analysis and deviation reporting; checklist completion is not causal validity or a safety gate.

title: Single-Case Design Technical Documentation
event: SCED design and visual evidence standards
notes: WWC phase-point rules are design-specific evidence-review standards and must not become a universal athlete baseline n or freshness threshold.

title: Waiting for baseline stability in single-case designs: Is it worth the time and effort?
notes: Challenges rigid baseline-stability waiting under specified simulations but does not permit ignoring trend or using a universal short baseline.

title: Some t-tests for N-of-1 trials with serial correlation
event: serially correlated level and rate contrasts
notes: Ordinary independent t-tests can misstate repeated-series inference; serial structure must be specified and very few observations remain unsafe for inference.

title: Single-case experimental designs: a systematic review of published research and current standards
event: SCED design measurement baseline and analysis practices
notes: SCED quality cannot be reduced to point count; measurement replication trend and analysis are separate gates and analytic disagreement remains.

title: Randomized Single-Case Experimental Designs in Healthcare Research: What, Why, and How?
event: randomized ABAB alternating-treatment multiple-baseline and changing-criterion designs
notes: Randomize start or order where feasible and analyze the actual assignment scheme; randomization does not fix poor measurement carryover or missingness.

title: Monitoring Athlete Training Loads: Consensus Statement
notes: Supports valid reliable consistent and contextual longitudinal monitoring; associations do not establish injury causation safety or a universal history.

title: Monitoring training load to understand fatigue in athletes
population: athletes across individual and team sports
notes: No single definitive fatigue marker exists; mixed markers require individualized contextual interpretation and cannot diagnose or clear training.

title: Monitoring the athlete training response: subjective self-reported measures trump commonly used objective measures: a systematic review
event: subjective and objective wellbeing response to training load
notes: Subjective and objective measures often disagree; preserve streams and provenance rather than fusing them into a causal readiness or safety score.

title: Single-subject research designs and data analyses for assessing elite athletes' conditioning
notes: Reviews AB reversal multiple-baseline and alternating-treatment designs for elite conditioning; serial dependency and applied design constraints remain.

title: Statistical Tests for Sports Science Practitioners: Identifying Performance Gains in Individual Athletes
population: four NCAA Division I basketball athletes
notes: Compares model statistic SWC CV and SEM in a narrow example; five sessions biweekly cadence and CV preference are not universal defaults.

title: Determining the Effects of a 6-Week Training Intervention on Reactive Strength: A Single-Case Experimental Design Approach
event: weekly reactive-strength testing with 5-7-week baseline and 6-week resistance-plyometric intervention
notes: Nonconcurrent multiple-baseline sport application shows individual paths; its phase durations are application details not universal n freshness efficacy or safety evidence.
